remember: Students will be able to recall the basic structures and functions of male and female reproductive systems.
Students will be able to list and explain the different phases of the menstrual cycle.
Students will be able to describe the process of fertilization and implantation.
understand: Students will be able to explain the role of hormones in controlling the reproductive cycle.
Students will be able to describe the process of spermatogenesis and oogenesis.
Students will be able to explain the process of embryonic development.
apply: Students will be able to explain the effects of external factors on the reproductive cycle.
Students will be able to describe the role of reproductive organs in disease processes.
Students will be able to apply knowledge of human reproduction to understand various health issues.
analyze: Students will be able to compare and contrast the reproductive systems of males and females.
Students will be able to analyze the role of genetics in human reproduction.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of environmental factors on human reproduction.

ncert_objectives: Explain the structure and function of male and female reproductive systems.
Describe the process of fertilization, formation of zygote, cleavage, morula, blastula, implantation and development of embryo and foetus.
Discuss the role of hormones in the regulation of reproductive functions in both males and females.
board_exam_objectives: Describe the structure of male and female reproductive systems.
Explain the process of spermatogenesis and oogenesis.
Discuss the menstrual cycle and its phases.
Describe the process of fertilization and implantation.
Explain the role of hormones in controlling the reproductive cycle.
